name: DeMario Douglas
headline: Buried on the depth chart, Douglas slides off the radar
body:
Douglas's 2025 line tells the story of a shrinking role: his snap share cratered to 25.9% from 62.6% the year prior, route participation fell to 37.6%, and target share dipped to 9.5% (46 targets across 16 games). When he was on the field he was efficient, posting a 0.612 EPA per play, a 56.5% success rate, and 2.16 yards per route run on just two drops. But the volume evaporated, and 64.8 fantasy points reflect a part-time contributor rather than a building block.

New England's offseason additions only worsen the outlook. Bringing in A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs pushes Douglas further down the pecking order, and his per-game targets have steadily slid toward the low single digits. The projections agree, pegging him near 23 catches for 271 yards and under 40 fantasy points. He's a deep-bench dart at an ADP outside the top 300, useful only as an in-season waiver flier if injuries open snaps ahead of him.
